messages.content: is there any overhead of empty interfaces in c#

No, there is no overhead of empty interfaces in C#. Empty interfaces have zero memory overhead because they do not have any defined members or properties. They are used primarily for specifying type constraints and casting. However, it is important to note that overuse of empty interfaces can lead to over-design and code maintainability problems.
so if i inherit from an empty interface with a struct, my struct will still have the same size and performance as without inheriting from that interface.

Yes, that is correct. Inheriting from an empty interface will not impact the performance or size of a struct. This is because interfaces in C# are purely a compile-time construct used to enforce contracts and provide a mechanism for polymorphism. Empty interfaces, in particular, do not define any members or contract to be enforced, therefore the struct will not incur any overhead as a result of implementing an empty interface. The size and performance of a struct will only be affected by the fields, properties, and methods it contains, not by the interfaces it implements.

what would you advise using for gamedev, a custom game engine or an existing game engine

Whether to use a custom game engine or an existing game engine for game development is a decision that depends on your particular needs and resources.

If you have a highly specific or unique vision for your game, or if your game requires a specialized technology that is not available in existing game engines, then it may be worth considering a custom game engine. Developing a custom game engine can give you greater control over the game mechanics, the rendering pipeline, and other aspects of the game development process. However, developing a custom game engine is a complex and time-consuming process that requires a team of experienced developers.

On the other hand, if you are working with a limited budget or a smaller team, or if your game requires less specialized functionality, then an existing game engine may be a better choice. Existing game engines can be customized and extended to meet your needs, and they often come with a wide range of tools and features that can help streamline the development process. Additionally, existing game engines have a large and active community of developers, which can be a valuable resource for learning and problem-solving.

Ultimately, the choice between a custom game engine versus an existing game engine is a decision that should be based on a careful assessment of your particular needs and resources.
chat gpt 4 told me that there is a minimal overhead when using empty interfaces for structs, is that true?

As far as I know, there is no actual overhead associated with an empty interface in C# when used with structs. Since an empty interface has no defined members or properties, it doesn't carry any information or metadata that would add any overhead to a struct that implements it. Therefore, using an empty interface with a struct in C# should not affect the size or performance of the struct. 

However, if the interface has methods or properties, it may impact performance and memory overhead compared to not using the interface, as the struct will have to implement these methods and carry metadata. It is generally considered a good practice to only use interfaces when they are needed for polymorphism or to enforce method contracts, and to avoid using empty interfaces unless absolutely necessary.
